comments.body:
## Reviewer's Guide Adds Accelerate live proof metadata, including a persisted PR body, a non-sensitive artifact description, and a privacy map that marks these files as export-approved artifacts for automated publication. #### Sequence diagram for Accelerate live proof artifact export ```mermaid sequenceDiagram actor Developer participant GitHub participant AccelerateService participant Repo Developer->>GitHub: push branch accelerate-live-proof GitHub->>GitHub: create pull_request GitHub->>AccelerateService: send pull_request_event AccelerateService->>Repo: fetch file .accelerate/status/privacy-map.yaml AccelerateService->>Repo: fetch file .accelerate/review/live-proof-pr-body.md AccelerateService->>Repo: fetch file .accelerate/review/live-proof-artifact.md AccelerateService->>AccelerateService: parse privacy_map and select export approved artifacts AccelerateService->>GitHub: post comment with live-proof-pr-body and live-proof-artifact GitHub->>Developer: display artifact comment on pull_request ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Introduce a persisted MD template describing the purpose and constraints of the Accelerate live proof PR. |
  • Create a markdown document that explains the live proof PR intent, including branch/PR operations and artifact behavior.
  • Document that the repository must remain available after the test to preserve proof artifacts.
| `.accelerate/review/live-proof-pr-body.md` | | Define privacy/export settings for Accelerate artifacts to explicitly allow publishing them as PR comments. |
  • Add a privacy-map configuration for Accelerate artifacts.
  • Mark the PR body markdown and a separate artifact markdown as `generated-export` and `export: approved` to indicate they are safe to publish.
| `.accelerate/status/privacy-map.yaml` | | Add a non-sensitive live proof artifact description and a placeholder file used for the live proof workflow. |
  • Create a markdown artifact describing that it is safe for public PR comments and contains no secrets.
  • Add an empty or placeholder text file used by the live proof process.
| `.accelerate/review/live-proof-artifact.md`
`accelerate-live-proof.txt` | ---
Tips and commands #### Interacting with Sourcery - **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request. - **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments. - **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it. - **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time. - **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to (re-)generate the summary at any time. - **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time. - **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore. - **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review! #### Customizing Your Experience Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to: - Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request summary, the reviewer's guide, and others. - Change the review language. - Add, remove or edit custom review instructions. - Adjust other review settings. #### Getting Help - [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback. - Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information. - Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

## Accelerate live proof artifact

Source artifact: `.accelerate/review/live-proof-artifact.md`

# Accelerate Live Proof Artifact

This artifact was generated for an Accelerate live GitHub adapter proof.

It contains no secrets and is safe to publish as a PR comment in the playground repository.

## Accelerate Closure Comment

Closure artifact: `.accelerate/review/live-proof-closure.md`

<!-- accelerate:closure-comment:v1 -->

# Accelerate Live Proof Closure

## Verification Proof

- repository creation: confirmed
- README initialization: confirmed
- branch push: confirmed
- PR creation: confirmed
- PR read/lookup: confirmed
- artifact comment attachment: confirmed
- rehydration packet: confirmed

## Self-review

This closure artifact contains no secrets and is approved for export to the playground PR.

## Residual Risks

- The playground repository is intentionally persistent and must not be deleted at the end of this test.
- Merge/land is not executed in this proof so the PR remains available for future sessions.
